title: Guarea trianae
taxonomy: Guarea trianae is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae. It is a member of the genus Guarea, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The Meliaceae family, also known as the mahogany family, consists of about 50 genera and over 600 species of plants. These plants are often found in tropical and subtropical regions worldwide, and are known for their distinctive flowers and fruits. The classification of Guarea trianae reflects its unique characteristics and its relationship to other plants within the Meliaceae family.
uses: Guarea trianae is a plant species known for its various medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. It is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antioxidant and antifungal activities, making it a potential natural remedy for several health conditions. In some regions, it is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal plant require further research and validation.
